mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the following week as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and real personal savings indicates a synchronized pullback in household balance sheets and consumption demand, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of HOUST and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10 in multivariate regression).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, rising industrial production signals capacity constraints that translate into higher corporate margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns during high FEDFUNDS periods, or predictive IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which mechanically supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no monotonic relationship with 5-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls growth and contraction in real savings rates indicates a shift from wealth-driven to income-driven consumption, supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and RSAFS contraction fails to explain variance in 20-day forward returns (F-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession environment, rising industrial production coupled with stable housing starts signals a supply-led recovery that drives short-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered windows.
